div.p: To all who shall view or hear this deed, Duncan Earl of Carrick sends Greeting: Know that we have given, granted, and by this our present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Blessed Mary in North Berwick, and to the monks serving God there, and in perpetuity to those who will serve there, three merks of silver to be received annually from the land of Berbeth, at two terms, that is half at the feast of Pentecost, and half at the feast of St Martin, until we have provided them with two-and-a-half pennylands in a suitable place. And that this our donation and grant, and the confirmation of our charters remain certain and constant, we have handed over the present deed to the same, secured with our seal. With these witnesses, the lord John, knight, son of the Earl, Master Hugh, Alan the dean, Mudoch the steward, and many others.
